summary:
Pantothenic acid (Vitamin B5) is synthesized in the human body through the mevalonate pathway in the liver. Unlike other listed vitamins such as niacin or cobalamins that require dietary intake, this one can be made internally by humans. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$

The summary should not contain any extra information beyond what was stated in Agent2’s response.

Pantothenic acid (vitamin B5) is synthesized in the human body via the mevalonate pathway, mainly in the liver. It is uniquely different among the options because it is produced endogenously rather than requiring dietary supplementation.
